mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p < 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production signal immediate supply chain bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions yield positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, rising input costs compress corporate margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in more than 65% of high-inflation months over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation shifts rather than genuine job creation, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) for 20-day returns over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth rates above 2.0% confirm sustained economic expansion and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 above 2.0% is followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of quarters over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, simultaneous declines in industrial production and retail sales signal demand destruction that outweighs supply constraints, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in INDPRO and RSAFS during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases.
